CapacityTaskId.description: The ID of the specified capacity task.

OutpostId.description: The ID of the Outpost associated with the specified capacity task.

OrderId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services Outposts order of the host associated with the capacity task.

AssetId.description: The ID of the asset. An Outpost asset can be a single server within an Outposts rack or an Outposts server configuration.

CreationDate.description: The date that the specified capacity task was created.

CompletionDate.description: The date that the specified capacity task successfully ran.

LastModifiedDate.description: The date that the specified capacity was last modified.

CatalogItemId.description: The ID of the catalog item.

ItemStatus.description: The status of a catalog item.

EC2Capacities.description: Information about the EC2 capacity of an item.

PowerKva.description: Information about the power draw of an item.

WeightLbs.description: The weight of the item in pounds.

SupportedUplinkGbps.description: The uplink speed this catalog item requires for the connection to the Region.

SupportedStorage.description: The supported storage options for the catalog item.

Status.description:
The status of the order.

  • PREPARING - Order is received and is being prepared.

  • IN_PROGRESS - Order is either being built, shipped, or installed. For more information, see the LineItem status.

  • COMPLETED - Order is complete.

  • CANCELLED - Order is cancelled.

  • ERROR - Customer should contact support.

The following statuses are deprecated: RECEIVED, PENDING, PROCESSING, INSTALLING, and FULFILLED.

LineItemCountsByStatus.description: The status of all line items in the order.

OrderSubmissionDate.description: The submission date for the order.

OrderFulfilledDate.description: The fulfilment date for the order.

OwnerId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the Outpost owner.

OutpostArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Outpost.

SiteArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the site.

AccountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account.

SiteArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the site.

OperatingAddressCountryCode.description: The ISO-3166 two-letter country code where the hardware is installed and powered on.

OperatingAddressStateOrRegion.description: State or region where the hardware is installed and powered on.

OperatingAddressCity.description: City where the hardware is installed and powered on.

RackPhysicalProperties.description: Information about the physical and logistical details for a rack at the site.

PowerDrawKva.description: The power draw available at the hardware placement position for the rack.

PowerPhase.description: The power option that you can provide for hardware.

UplinkGbps.description: The uplink speed the rack supports for the connection to the Region.

UplinkCount.description: The number of uplinks each Outpost network device.

FiberOpticCableType.description: The type of fiber used to attach the Outpost to the network.

OpticalStandard.description: The type of optical standard used to attach the Outpost to the network. This field is dependent on uplink speed, fiber type, and distance to the upstream device. For more information about networking requirements for racks, see Network in the Amazon Web Services Outposts User Guide.

MaximumSupportedWeightLbs.description: The maximum rack weight that this site can support. NO_LIMIT is over 2000 lbs (907 kg).

OutpostId.description: The ID of the Outpost in the order.

Status.description:
The status of the order.

  • PREPARING - Order is received and being prepared.

  • IN_PROGRESS - Order is either being built or shipped. To get more details, see the line item status.

  • DELIVERED - Order was delivered to the Outpost site.

  • COMPLETED - Order is complete.

  • CANCELLED - Order is cancelled.

  • ERROR - Customer should contact support.

The following status are deprecated: RECEIVED, PENDING, PROCESSING, INSTALLING, and FULFILLED.

OutpostId.description: ID of the Outpost associated with the specified capacity task.

OrderId.description: ID of the Amazon Web Services Outposts order associated with the specified capacity task.

AssetId.description: The ID of the Outpost asset. An Outpost asset can be a single server within an Outposts rack or an Outposts server configuration.

RequestedInstancePools.description: List of instance pools requested in the capacity task.

InstancesToExclude.description: Instances that the user specified they cannot stop in order to free up the capacity needed to run the capacity task.

DryRun.description: Performs a dry run to determine if you are above or below instance capacity.

CapacityTaskStatus.description:
Status of the capacity task.

A capacity task can have one of the following statuses:

  • REQUESTED - The capacity task was created and is awaiting the next step by Amazon Web Services Outposts.

  • IN_PROGRESS - The capacity task is running and cannot be cancelled.

  • FAILED - The capacity task could not be completed.

  • COMPLETED - The capacity task has completed successfully.

  • WAITING_FOR_EVACUATION - The capacity task requires capacity to run. You must stop the recommended EC2 running instances to free up capacity for the task to run.

  • CANCELLATION_IN_PROGRESS - The capacity task has been cancelled and is in the process of cleaning up resources.

  • CANCELLED - The capacity task is cancelled.

CreationDate.description: The date the capacity task was created.

CompletionDate.description: The date the capacity task ran successfully.

LastModifiedDate.description: The date the capacity task was last modified.

TaskActionOnBlockingInstances.description:
User-specified option in case an instance is blocking the capacity task from running. Shows one of the following options:

  • WAIT_FOR_EVACUATION - Checks every 10 minutes over 48 hours to determine if instances have stopped and capacity is available to complete the task.

  • FAIL_TASK - The capacity task fails.

Count.description: The number of instances for the specified instance type.

OwnerId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID of the Outpost owner.

OutpostArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Outpost.

SiteArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the site.

Subscriptions.description: The subscription details for the specified Outpost.

ContractEndDate.description: The date the current contract term ends for the specified Outpost. You must start the renewal or decommission process at least 5 business days before the current term for your Amazon Web Services Outposts ends. Failing to complete these steps at least 5 business days before the current term ends might result in unanticipated charges.

SubscriptionId.description: The ID of the subscription that appears on the Amazon Web Services Billing Center console.

SubscriptionType.description:
The type of subscription which can be one of the following:

  • ORIGINAL - The first order on the Amazon Web Services Outposts.

  • RENEWAL - Renewal requests, both month to month and longer term.

  • CAPACITY_INCREASE - Capacity scaling orders.

SubscriptionStatus.description:
The status of subscription which can be one of the following:

  • INACTIVE - Subscription requests that are inactive.

  • ACTIVE - Subscription requests that are in progress and have an end date in the future.

  • CANCELLED - Subscription requests that are cancelled.

MonthlyRecurringPrice.description: The amount you are billed each month in the subscription period.

UpfrontPrice.description: The amount billed when the subscription is created. This is a one-time charge.

OutpostArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Outpost.

VCPUs.description: The number of default VCPUs in an instance type.
The number of default VCPUs in an instance type.

For more information about networking requirements for racks, see Network in the Amazon Web Services Outposts User Guide.

MaximumSupportedWeightLbs.description: The maximum rack weight that this site can support. NO_LIMIT is over 2000 lbs (907 kg).

AddressType.description: The type of the address you receive.

DistrictOrCounty.description: The district or county for the address.

CountryCode.description: The ISO-3166 two-letter country code for the address.

AssetInstances.description: List of instances owned by all accounts on the Outpost. Does not include Amazon EBS or Amazon S3 instances.

AssetId.description: The ID of the asset. An Outpost asset can be a single server within an Outposts rack or an Outposts server configuration.

AccountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account.

AwsServiceName.description: The Amazon Web Services service name of the instance.

AssetId.description: The ID of the asset. An Outpost asset can be a single server within an Outposts rack or an Outposts server configuration.

RackId.description: The rack ID of the asset.

HostId.description: The host ID of the Dedicated Host on the asset.

State.description:
The state.

  • ACTIVE - The asset is available and can provide capacity for new compute resources.

  • ISOLATED - The asset is undergoing maintenance and can't provide capacity for new compute resources. Existing compute resources on the asset are not affected.

  • RETIRING - The underlying hardware for the asset is degraded. Capacity for new compute resources is reduced. Amazon Web Services sends notifications for resources that must be stopped before the asset can be replaced.

InstanceFamilies.description: A list of the names of instance families that are currently associated with a given asset.

InstanceTypeCapacities.description: The instance type capacities configured for this asset. This can be changed through a capacity task.

MaxVcpus.description: The maximum number of vCPUs possible for the specified asset.

AssetLocation.description: The position of an asset in a rack.

RackElevation.description: The position of an asset in a rack measured in rack units.

BlockingInstances.description: A list of all running Amazon EC2 instances on the Outpost. Stopping one or more of these instances can free up the capacity needed to run the capacity task.

AccountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account.

AwsServiceName.description: The Amazon Web Services service name that owns the specified blocking instance.

CapacityTaskId.description: ID of the capacity task that you want to start.

OutpostId.description: ID of the Outpost associated with the capacity task.

OrderId.description: ID of the Amazon Web Services Outposts order of the host associated with the capacity task.

AssetId.description: The ID of the asset. An Outpost asset can be a single server within an Outposts rack or an Outposts server configuration.

RequestedInstancePools.description: List of the instance pools requested in the specified capacity task.

InstancesToExclude.description: User-specified instances that must not be stopped in order to free up the capacity needed to run the capacity task.

DryRun.description: Results of the dry run showing if the specified capacity task is above or below the available instance capacity.

Failed.description: Reason that the specified capacity task failed.

CreationDate.description: Date that the specified capacity task was created.

CompletionDate.description: Date that the specified capacity task ran successfully.

LastModifiedDate.description: Date that the specified capacity task was last modified.

TaskActionOnBlockingInstances.description:
User-specified option in case an instance is blocking the capacity task from running.

  • WAIT_FOR_EVACUATION - Checks every 10 minutes over 48 hours to determine if instances have stopped and capacity is available to complete the task.

  • FAIL_TASK - The capacity task fails.
